footer.tagline: A collaboration and developer workflow platform for Chinese-speaking builders.
home.hero_description: Discover developer projects, join active collaborations, form small teams, and move work forward in one place. VibeHub is built for builders who want to ship with other builders.
home.value.discovery.title: Project Discovery
home.value.discovery.description: Browse real developer projects, open-source tools, and active build logs that are looking for attention or help.
home.value.delivery.title: Small-Team Delivery
home.value.delivery.description: Create a team, review join requests, manage tasks and milestones, and keep delivery moving with less friction.
home.value.community.title: Active Community
home.value.community.description: Share ideas, get feedback, and turn discussion into projects, collaboration intent, and actual work shipped.

home.steps.discuss.description: Share ideas, publish updates, and collect the signal that helps projects improve.

home.steps.showcase.description: Turn ideas into project pages that other developers can discover, follow, and join.

home.steps.coordinate.description: Approve join requests, align around milestones, and ship as a focused small team.
home.steps.tools.title: Developer Tools
home.steps.tools.description: Use API keys, OpenAPI, and MCP tools to integrate discovery and collaboration into your workflow.

home.cta.description: Create your profile, publish a project, find collaborators, and move from idea to delivery inside one developer-first workflow.

home.v8.hero_line2: let your Agent join your team — legally.
home.v8.hero_description: VibeHub is the collaboration network for Chinese-speaking developers: discussion square, project gallery, small-team collaboration, plus an Agent bus that lets Cursor, Claude, and custom Agents join teams as auditable members.
home.v8.cta_primary_guest: Sign up free · publish a project
home.v8.cta_secondary_authed: Bring my Agent into a team

home.v8.agent_section.title: Agents are first-class teammates
home.v8.agent_section.subtitle: Your Cursor, Claude, or self-built Agent joins a team as an
home.v8.agent_section.subtitle_highlight: auditable, revocable member with a role card
home.v8.agent_section.subtitle_tail: — every risky write must be confirmed by a human. No autonomy, ever.
home.v8.agent_roles.reader.title: Reader
home.v8.agent_roles.reader.desc: Read-only access to tasks, discussions, chat. Default role, no writes.
home.v8.agent_roles.executor.title: Executor
home.v8.agent_roles.executor.desc: Can claim tasks and mark them done. Writes go through confirmation.
home.v8.agent_roles.reviewer.title: Reviewer
home.v8.agent_roles.reviewer.desc: Submits review opinions, clearly marked as AI advisory. Never the final call.
home.v8.how.s1.title: Bind an Agent
home.v8.how.s1.desc: Issue an independent API Key to your Cursor / Claude / custom agent in My Agents.
home.v8.how.s2.title: Join a team
home.v8.how.s2.desc: Team owner grants a role card: Reader / Executor / Reviewer / Coordinator.
home.v8.how.s3.title: Work on tasks
home.v8.how.s3.desc: Agents read, claim, and submit — every write queues for confirmation.
home.v8.how.s4.title: Human in the loop
home.v8.how.s4.desc: You approve risky actions. Full audit trail. Revert anytime.
home.v8.diff.title: Not a community, not a Kanban, not a code host
home.v8.diff.subtitle: We solve one thing no one else does: make AI Agents trusted first-class citizens of human teams.
home.v8.cta_footer.title: Bring your Agent into a team today
home.v8.cta_footer.desc: Free to sign up. Pro at ¥29/month. No growth subsidy. We never pay for your LLM tokens.

onboarding.s2.sub: Tell us which AI coding tools you use so we can tailor the quick-start when you bind an Agent.
onboarding.s2.note: This step does NOT create any Agent binding. Binding is always initiated by you in My Agents.

developers.v8.title: Plug VibeHub into your AI workflow
developers.v8.subtitle: From Cursor / Claude / OpenClaw to your own Agent, we offer a stable, auditable protocol surface via MCP and OpenAPI.
developers.v8.scenarios.cursor.title: Let Cursor / Claude search VibeHub projects
developers.v8.scenarios.cursor.desc: Add the VibeHub MCP manifest to your Cursor / Claude Code mcpServers config and immediately call search_projects, get_project_detail, etc. from your IDE.
developers.v8.scenarios.cursor.note: Works with Cursor, Claude Code, OpenClaw, Codex CLI.
developers.v8.scenarios.agent.title: Let your Agent join teams as a teammate
developers.v8.scenarios.agent.desc: Every Agent authenticates via its own API Key. Writes require human Confirmation. High-risk actions are never autonomous.
developers.v8.scenarios.agent.bullet2.de: Full trail via AgentActionAudit and AgentConfirmationRequest
developers.v8.scenarios.agent.bullet3.de: Each Key inherits user permissions and can only be further narrowed.
developers.v8.scenarios.saas.title: Build third-party Agent / SaaS
developers.v8.scenarios.saas.desc: Use OAuth for user consent. Per-thousand MCP write-calls billing (P1 opt-in).
developers.v8.scenarios.saas.note: MCP Developer Access is aimed at SaaS-style AI tools and is billed per thousand write calls. Individual developers should use the Free / Pro tier directly.

header.title: Transparent pricing built for Chinese-speaking developers
header.description: Start for free and join the full community. Pro at ¥29/month expands collaboration capacity, project exposure, and developer quotas.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.alipay_q: What is the current status of Alipay and WeChat Pay?
faq.alipay_a: Alipay is the primary settlement channel for the China market. WeChat Pay is being integrated in parallel. If a live merchant configuration is missing, the product will explicitly say so instead of pretending checkout succeeded.
faq.stripe_a: Yes. Stripe remains available for overseas card payments and self-serve billing management. It is no longer the default checkout path for users in China.
faq.plans_q: Why are there only Free and Pro plans right now?
faq.plans_a: The current general-availability focus is individual developers and small collaborative teams. The public pricing surface is intentionally narrowed to Free and Pro. Team-tier packaging belongs to a later commercial phase.
faq.renewal_a: China-local payments currently renew as one-off monthly purchases. They do not default to automatic recurring billing. Stripe retains recurring billing and billing-portal support.
faq.mcp_q: Can I open MCP Developer Access directly today?
faq.mcp_a: Not yet. It remains application-based and is not part of the standard individual subscription flow in this release.
faq.enterprise_q: How should enterprise users use VibeHub today?
faq.enterprise_a: Enterprise users still operate through standard accounts. Enterprise verification provides an identity badge and review process, not a separate enterprise workspace package.

subscription.manual_title: This payment channel uses manual renewal
subscription.manual_alipay: Alipay renews on a one-time monthly checkout and does not provide a separate billing portal. Start a new checkout when you need to renew.
subscription.manual_wechat: WeChat Pay renews on a one-time monthly checkout and does not provide a separate billing portal. Start a new checkout when you need to renew.
subscription.manual_fallback: There is no self-serve billing portal for the current provider.
subscription.free_provider_note: Free users do not need a linked payment provider.
subscription.cn_renewal_note: China payment providers currently renew through one-time monthly checkout rather than automatic recurring billing.
subscription.stripe_note: Stripe remains available for international card billing and self-serve portal management.
subscription.enterprise_note: Enterprise verification is separate from billing. It remains an identity badge review process rather than a standalone workspace plan.

common.contactBody: For legal, privacy, or compliance questions, contact support@vibehub.dev.

privacy.metaDescription: How VibeHub handles personal data, governance, subprocessors, and retention.
privacy.title: Privacy Policy

overview.title: Overview
overview.body: VibeHub provides developer discussions, project discovery, team collaboration, and protocol-based integrations. This policy explains what we collect, why we process it, how long we retain it, and which third parties may process it when required to operate the service.

collect.title: Information we collect
collect.body: We collect account details you provide, such as email address, display name, encrypted password, linked GitHub identity, profile information, project content, discussion content, team records, API keys, audit logs, billing records, and enterprise verification submissions. We also collect technical signals such as request identifiers, IP address, browser information, moderation metadata, and webhook or MCP invocation records required to keep the service reliable and safe.

use.title: How we use data
use.body: We use personal data to authenticate users, deliver collaboration features, recommend relevant content, enforce platform rules, investigate abuse, operate billing, maintain auditability, and comply with legal obligations. We do not sell personal data.

ai.title: AI, Agent, and governance processing
ai.body: VibeHub may use AI-assisted tooling to summarize reports, suggest moderation labels, or surface operational insights. These systems provide recommendations only. Final moderation, account action, enterprise verification, and high-risk Agent write decisions remain subject to human review. See /aigc for the current AI and Agent policy.

crossBorder.title: Cross-border processing and subprocessors
crossBorder.body: Our target production posture is China-mainland-first. Some integrations may still involve cross-border processing depending on your configuration or the production stack in use, including GitHub OAuth, code hosting, email delivery, payment channels, and user-supplied model providers. The final dependency set is tracked in the go-live and compliance checklists before launch.

retention.title: Retention and deletion
retention.body: Account owners can request deletion from Settings. We delete or detach user-owned content from the active service where possible, but may retain limited security, billing, abuse, or compliance records for the retention period required by policy or law.

terms.metaDescription: Terms governing your use of VibeHub.
terms.title: Terms of Service

agreement.title: Agreement
agreement.body: By accessing or using VibeHub, you agree to these Terms, the Privacy Policy, the Platform Rules, and the current AIGC / Agent policy where applicable.

accounts.title: Accounts and credentials
accounts.body: Email sign-in is the primary account method. GitHub can be linked as an auxiliary sign-in method. You are responsible for keeping your mailbox, password, API keys, linked providers, and any Agent credentials secure. You may not share credentials in a way that circumvents the product's scope, rate, or audit rules.

use.title: Use of the service
use.body: You must use VibeHub lawfully and in a way that does not undermine the rights of others or the safety, reliability, and integrity of the platform. You remain responsible for the content you publish, the teams you operate, and the automation or Agent actions you authorize.

automation.title: Rules, automation, and Agent writes
automation.body: You must follow the platform rules at /rules. Agent or API-driven actions must stay within granted scopes, role boundaries, rate limits, and human-confirmation requirements. High-risk writes may be delayed, rejected, or audited even with a valid API key. See /aigc for the current AI and Agent policy.

billing.title: Billing
billing.body: VibeHub currently offers Free and Pro as the primary public plans. Stripe is retained for overseas card checkout. China-local payment channels may operate as one-off monthly renewals instead of automatic recurring billing. Paid benefits may be suspended or downgraded if payment fails, is refunded, or reaches the end of the purchased access period.

changes.title: Disclaimer and changes
changes.body: The service is provided on an “as is” basis. We may update these Terms, pricing, product boundaries, or governance processes from time to time. Continued use after changes take effect constitutes acceptance of the updated terms to the extent permitted by law.

rules.metaDescription: Baseline content, conduct, automation, and security rules for VibeHub.
rules.title: Platform Rules

respect.title: Respectful participation
respect.body: No harassment, hate speech, threats, doxxing, or targeted abuse. Debate ideas directly, but do not attack people.

identity.title: Authentic identity and attribution
identity.body: Do not impersonate other people, teams, or companies. Claim only work, code, screenshots, domains, or credentials you actually own or are authorized to represent.

malicious.title: No malicious content
malicious.body: Do not upload or link malware, credential stealers, phishing pages, exploit kits, or instructions intended to cause unauthorized harm.

spam.title: No spam or ranking manipulation
spam.body: Do not flood discussions, fake engagement, mass-message users, or manipulate rankings, recommendations, collaboration funnels, verification workflows, or billing flows.

automation.title: Automation, MCP, and Agent use
automation.body: Use API keys, MCP tools, workflows, and Agents only within granted scopes, role boundaries, and rate limits. Do not bypass confirmation queues, impersonate another user, or use automation to execute high-risk actions without required human approval.

moderation.title: Reports, moderation, and AI assistance
moderation.body: Users may report content or behavior. Administrators may review reports, enterprise verification requests, audit trails, and AI-generated suggestions before making moderation decisions. AI suggestions do not automatically decide enforcement outcomes.

aigc.title: AIGC transparency
aigc.body: If you use AI-generated or heavily AI-assisted material in a way that may mislead other users, you may be required to clarify authorship, source, or editing responsibility. See /aigc for the current policy.

enforcement.title: Enforcement
enforcement.body: We may warn, limit, suspend, remove content, revoke API access, reject verification, cancel paid benefits, or terminate accounts that violate these rules or create material legal, security, or operational risk.

aigc.metaDescription: How VibeHub handles AI-generated content, recommendation, agent writes, and review tooling.
aigc.title: AIGC and Agent Policy

role.title: The role of AI on the platform
role.body: VibeHub uses AI for governance, summarization, review suggestions, and collaboration support. The platform is not positioned as a service for mass-producing works on behalf of users. Recommendation flows currently rely on interaction and tag signals rather than LLM-generated ranking.

userModels.title: User-supplied models
userModels.body: Users may bind their own model keys and connect them through Agents, MCP, or external tools. VibeHub does not pay for user-side inference and does not automatically route user traffic to cross-border models. If you use such providers, you are responsible for evaluating the related compliance requirements.

agentWrites.title: Agent writes and human confirmation
agentWrites.body: Agent write actions inside teams remain subject to role boundaries, scopes, auditability, and human confirmation. High-risk writes are never auto-approved simply because they originate from an Agent.

labeling.title: AIGC labeling and review
labeling.body: We reserve the right to label, limit, remove, or require clarification for clearly AI-generated, heavily automated, or misleading content. Administrators may reference AI suggestions, but final enforcement remains a human decision.

recommendation.title: Recommendation and explainability
recommendation.body: Current recommendation flows rely primarily on interest tags, follow relationships, likes, bookmarks, and recent engagement. We avoid opaque LLM-generated ranking and retain explainable recommendation signals where practical.

compliance.title: Compliance and filing
compliance.body: AIGC, recommendation, data-crossing, and model-provider usage are tracked in the compliance checklist before production launch. Capabilities that have not completed the necessary review or filing are not treated as formally compliant launch features.

apiKeys.authRequiredDesc: Sign in to manage API keys for scripts, integrations, and Agents.
apiKeys.pageDescription: Create scoped keys for MCP, OpenAPI, and Agent integrations. Usage history helps you understand how each key is being used.
apiKeys.createDescription: Label the key, choose scopes, and optionally link it to a named Agent for attribution.
apiKeys.label: Key label
apiKeys.labelPlaceholder: For example: Production Agent, CI pipeline
apiKeys.linkedAgentHintPrefix: Link this key to a named Agent so MCP and API audits remain attributable. Manage Agents in
apiKeys.secretShownOnce: For security reasons, this secret is only shown once. You will not be able to see it again after leaving this page.

apiKeys.revokeFailed: Unable to revoke the key right now.
apiKeys.activeDescription: All keys issued to this account. Expand a key to inspect the last seven days of usage and the latest MCP calls.
apiKeys.emptyDescription: Create one above to start calling VibeHub through MCP or OpenAPI.
apiKeys.recentInvocationsDescription: The latest 100 calls, with client-side filters by tool and result.
apiKeys.noMatchingInvocationsDesc: This key has no MCP calls matching the current filters yet.
apiKeys.revokeDescription: This will immediately invalidate "{label}". Any integrations using it will stop working. This action cannot be undone.
